programmes.show_description: 1956. Comedy Western. A corset store owner goes broke and becomes a traveling sales lady in Texas 1897. Starring Ginger Rogers, Barry Nelson and a young Clint Eastwood.
1965. A compelling look at some of the independent and local government-sponsored nursery schools in Britain.
1963. Who Killed Cable Roberts? Directed by Jeffrey Hayden. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. A legendary writer, documentary filmmaker, and hunter, is found murdered in his study.
1965. Death of a Fleming. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
Highlander. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Rufus Cruickshank. Duncan, a highland visitor to the outlaw camp, is viewed with suspicion by Little John.
Before The Mast. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Ferdy Mayne & Roger Snowdon. When a pirate ship is seen in the waters around New Providence, Tempest hatches a daring plan to destroy the ship.
The Suspect. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Tell must prove a girl innocent of giving information to the Austrians about secret arms shipments.
Coffin Sam. Director: Aaron Spelling. Stars Don Durant. Sam, a gunslinger, claims his gun hand is becoming crippled. Sam offers Ringo an interesting proposition to help aid his reputation.
1954. Crime. Directed by John Gilling. Stars Charles Victor, Peggy Mount and Michael Craig. An elderly bank cashier with two years to live absconds with the contents of a bank safe.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Having a Baby
programmes.show_description: 1964. A remarkable look at the medical and social services available for both hospital and home births.
1960. Drama. Directed by Don Sharp. Stars Carol White, Alan Rothwell & Lois Daine. A bored teenager finds excitement in the life of crime, but his interests change when he starts to fall in love.
1971. Western. Directed by Ted Post. Stars Clint Walker, Kathryn Hays & Edgar Buchanan. Tough U.S. marshal Dave Harmon is sent out to Yuma, Arizona to clean up the lawless western town.
1958. Crime Thriller. Directed by Paul Rotha with Lee Patterson. The daughter of a convicted and executed killer believes she has murdered blackmailer Hilton Edwards.
Long Live the King. 1962. Stars Dermot Walsh & Lisa Daniely. Prince Richard is unaware that his father the King is ill, a messenger is sent to him, but a scheming Prince John sends Richard astray.
1957. Drama. Director: Compton Bennett. Stars Phyllis Kirk, Dan O'Herlihy & Wilfrid Hyde-White. Robberies and murder take place in a quiet town and the investigators have a lot of questions.
1944. Drama. Directed by Basil Dearden. Starring Mervyn Johns, Glynis Johns & Tom Walls. A group of travellers, all with something to hide in their past, take shelter from a storm in an old inn.

programmes.show_name: Sherlock Holmes: The Sign of Four
programmes.show_description: 1987. Thriller. Director: Peter Hammond. Stars Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Jenny Seagrove. A young lady contacts Sherlock Holmes for his help regarding her father who disappeared 10 years ago.
A Corpse in Room 13. 1955. Stars Margaret Anderson & Phil Brown. A wealthy gentleman checks into a hotel and ends up dead. In financial desperation, the hotelier makes a series of bad decisions.
Mike and Noel head back to 1955 for a very prestigious car race, the fabulous Petula Clark sings in a brilliant new Scopitone and take a peek at late 1960s life!
1989. Stars Diana Rigg, James Wilby & David McCallum. Angie and Kit's secret has been hidden for so long. It is only a matter of time until Helena finds out about their betrayal.
1946. War. Director Peter Ustinov. Stars Ralph Richardson and Richard Attenborough. During World War II, on the eve of the Battle of Britain, British scientists develop the first radar systems.
1974. Comedy. Directed by John Robins. Stars Benny Hill & Patricia Hayes. A brilliant collection of sketches and musical numbers taken from Benny Hill's popular British television show.
The Resident Patient. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read in which Blessington seeks help from Sherlock Holmes after discovering intruders have been in his bedroom.
And in Again. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie gives Charlie Endell a birthday present, but Charlie shows his appreciation in a very peculiar way.
1938. War. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Barry K. Barnes, Sophie Stewart & Jack Hawkins. During World War One, a number of captured British officers attempt to escape a prisoner-of-war camp.
All Good Friends: The Case of the Handless Corpse. 1992. Stars Suzanne Packer & Michelle Chadwick. Based on the 1979 case, where two divers found the body of a man in a flooded Lancashire quarry.
1965. A Million Bucks In Anybody's Language. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Honey investigates the death of a P.I.
Deputy Sheriff. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Maurice Marsac & Celia Lovsky. Jim volunteers to become a deputy sheriff to protect a prisoner, the son of a French society member, from mob violence.
1937. Drama. Director: Syd Courtenay. Stars Peggy Simpson, Ian Fleming & Tod Slaughter. A story focusing on a woman's passion for an idle man and her marriage to his uncle, the family benefactor.
1951. Mystery. Directed by Francis Searle. Stars Brian Reece, Joy Shelton & Christine Norden. PC 49 teams up with his girlfriend to solve a crime after a murder is committed right under his nose.
1952. Comedy. In this British classic, the Pickwick Club send a group to find interesting things around England. Stars James Hayter, Nigel Patrick, Kathleen Harrison & Harry Fowler.
A short travelogue describing the history of Kent in 1954, with a look at the towns that made up the Cinque Ports.
1963. Crime Drama. Director: Lance Comfort. When writer Greg Parker (Tony Britton) goes on holiday, little does he expect to be lodging in the same B&B as an escaped murderer (William Lucas).
The Emperor's Hat. 1958. Stars Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. William is forced to show his renowned marksmanship with the crossbow by shooting an apple off his son's head.
1960. Comedy. Directed by Ralph Thomas. Stars Leslie Phillips, Joan Sims & Fenella Fielding. Richard Hare's love life has as many ups and downs, as does his career.
1959. The Ghost Train Murder. Directed by Geoffrey Muller. Starring Russell Napier, Gorgon Needham and Mary Laura Wood. A Hungarian girl is found murdered; stabbed by knitting needles. (S1, E31)
1940. The ministry of supply launches a nationwide campaign to prevent at home waste burning and encourage sorting of waste. This rubbish can be reused for a profit.
1948. Drama. Directors: Ken Annakin & Michael C Chorlton. Stars Phyllis Calvert, James Donald & David Tomlinson. A plane crash lands miles from civilisation. The passengers must prevail.
Episode in Bathurst. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Chuck Faulkner & Joe McCormick. Cobb's stages are halted outside Bathurst until he agrees to pay toll to gunmen who have taken over the town.
The Dove Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo steals a dove-shaped medal from the leader of a European nation. The medal has information about Thrush.
The Reluctant Witness. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. Some neighborhood thugs who run a stolen car ring beat a man to death. A timid shop girl is the only witness.
Murders in Batz - Les Blessures de l¿île. 2014. Director: Edwin Baily. Stars Stéphane Freiss & Flore Bonaventura. An inspector investigates the island's first murder in a century as storm hits.
Join the fabulous Caroline Munro down in the Cellar Club. Tonight, we start with The World of Hammer episode Dracula and the Undead, which explores classic Hammer films featuring Dracula.
Dracula and the Undead. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at how Hammer defined Dracula as a seductive icon of forbidden desire and everlasting evil with clips from a number of classic films.
1959. Horror. Directed by Bernard L. Kowalski. Stars Ken Clark, Yvette Vickers & Jan Shepard. A man discovers giant leeches are behind several recent disappearances, but will anyone believe him?
